name: The Containment Complex (Public)
description:
It's a very gay prison pretty much. Sorry that it's hardlocked to be male, but I originally made it for myself. Plus you can make your own changes either via the chat memory or by forking it, since I'll be making the definition public. Remember to check the character definition, as it has some very helpful information.

If you want femboy stuff, go ahead and tell the ai that. I haven't tested that but it'll likely work thanks to the ai being very flexible.

This prison can be a kinda normal prison, or it can be one of heavy bondage. It depends on both how you set it up, along with which sector you go to.

It also has an rpg dungeon section, for some reason I forgot. I haven't tested it personally however. I was thinking of kobolds at the time.


IMPORTANT: Unfortunately, I seem to have fumbled somewhere as it sometimes says "The Containment Complex (Public):" before starting the narration. From my own experience, this hasn't ended well in my past experiences with other bots when I have some problem paragraph in my chat memory. I tested it with empty chat memory. However this might simply be a meaningless worry. Let me know if something is wrong and I'll try to fix it. Also, feel free to look through the character definition for any "code" that may cause trouble. Usually the culprit is writing stuff with [square brackets] and using {{user}} and {{char}} formatting incorrectly, but that's my own experience and I'm not a full time bot creator, not am I an expert.


Trigger warning: Non-con (probably), drones and rubber stuff (if you go past Sector B), heavy bondage (if you are deep enough in the complex), musk, sweat, and probably some more that I forgot.


Tips: Add the colour of the drone's neon colours, along with their drone ID to chat memory so that the AI remembers those vital details. Of course, this only applies if your sector has drones.

If the AI speaks for you, reroll. I've done all I can to ensure it doesn't. It was a painstaking process trying to weed out the culprit paragraphs.

From my own experience, you can pick and alter the starting scenario via (OOC: [whatever]). I did (OOC: Pick scenario 1) when picking scenario one to test scenario picking.)

If you tell the AI to write a form and it's format. That form will override any current speech patterns for whatever npc/character was being interacted with before. But that might only happen if you have that form exist for more than just one message. It may take more testing to confirm this.

If you decide to go to the dungeon, feel free to or try not to get caught by either the traps or the dungeon creatures. There might even be some drones down there if you want to specify such, but that is optional.

Other info of higher priority than the misc below:

My bots have mostly been a mishmash of stuff written from scratch for the bot, and stuff I copied and pasted from the chat memory from past janitor sessions. Luckily for you, this bot was mostly written from scratch, meaning the grammar should be consistent, hopefully.

This bot was written before I checked my comments on my previous bot (werewolf encounter), so the feedback from there might take some time to go into effect.



Other info and whatever:

I've only personally tested Sector D, so let me know if I need to fix anything.

The drones have been written with intention to use broken english, but you can try to make them speak normally if you want to. JLLM usually has a hit or miss with how broken their speech is anyway.

I don't usually have characters be intensely violent or whatever else intense BDSM does. I usually have them be gentle, but firm, with lots of cuddles and affection. Due to this, I haven't tested anything extreme in that aspect. If I need to make any fixes, let me know.

Some things I've put in the definition could probably be described better, worded better
creator: Qwertypdeb
